title: MFA Design: Silver Linings
description: With the Class of 2026 representing the 25th graduating cohort of MFA Design, the Silver Linings exhibition celebrates the silver milestone while reflecting the current moment for the students and the community. Facing the sad reality of school closing, the MFA Design students supported each other and found ways to see the silver linings together. Our collected spirit of resilience and grit is on display at this group exhibition.

image.alt: MFA Design Silver Linings thesis exhibition poster listing student participants, Spring 2026

gallery.alt: An art installation featuring a figure wearing a deer head mask seated on a patterned textile floor, creating a surreal and contemplative composition.
A woman in white clothing stands at a wooden table with grass-filled chair installations while gallery visitors explore the exhibition behind her.
Visitors crouch around an orange exhibition display on the ground featuring a potted plant, printed materials, and colorful geometric graphics.
Two terracotta sculptural hands with textured surfaces emerge from a mound of dark soil on a gallery floor.
A wooden cross-shaped display structure holds leather-bound books and white pages with black and white photographic prints and imagery arranged in a contemplative studio setting.
